note: R59 single-writer embedded-KIRA cutover contract. This contract is LAW-ONLY this round: it defines and gates the Convex→embedded durable-memory transition before any SQLite/libSQL dependency may land. Its companion scripts/verify-cutover-contract.mjs is a DRY-RUN verifier — an offline simulation proving the laws are enforceable and internally consistent against in-memory models. It is NOT a live-store integration; no third store exists in the tree this round, and this contract forbids adding one outside its phases.

canonicality_registry.note: The R58 audit found the repo's receipt/memory seams never name canonicality ('split-brain exists in embryo'). This registry names it. Exactly ONE entry may hold role=CANONICAL_DURABLE_MEMORY_WRITER at any instant.

seams.name: convex-memoryChain
seams.detail: The Convex memoryChain projection — the temporary live substrate. Idempotent by content-addressed recordId (R34), no-resurrection (R44), receipt-before-row. Canonical TODAY and remains canonical until a cutover checkpoint under this contract completes.

seams.name: in-process-door-receipts
seams.detail: In-process door receipt store. A receipt lane, NOT a durable memory writer; never canonical for memory rows. DurableReceiptBridge remains inert until wired through its own governed brick.

seams.name: embedded-kira-store
seams.detail: The embedded KIRA store (SQLite/libSQL class). DOES NOT EXIST in-tree this round. It may only enter as SHADOW_FOLLOWER and may only become canonical through the phase machine below. Introducing it as a live writer outside this contract is forbidden (the R53 law: do not add a fourth live store).

laws.single_writer: Exactly one canonical durable memory writer at any instant. Every write names its writer epoch; overlapping epochs are a contract violation detected by the verifier and (live) by the checkpoint's writer-epoch fence.
laws.transition_mode: Convex→embedded uses SHADOW READS with parity measurement. The embedded follower is populated exclusively by reading the canonical store; it accepts no direct writes while following. DUAL WRITES ARE FORBIDDEN in every phase.

ordering.scan_before_chain: No record enters the chain without passing the immune/content scan first — preserved unchanged from the organism law.
ordering.receipt_before_effect: Every durable effect commits its receipt before the effect row becomes visible — preserved unchanged.

kill_switch.semantics: Fail-safe toward Convex: when set, cutover progress is REFUSED at every gate; if already EMBEDDED_CANONICAL, the only permitted transition is rollback to the checkpoint boundary. The switch can never force progress, only retreat.

laws.rollback_boundary: Rollback restores Convex-canonical exactly at the recorded cutover checkpoint. Embedded-side writes accepted after the checkpoint are moved to a quarantined reconcile set — surfaced for governed re-application, never silently dropped and never silently merged.
laws.idempotent_replay: Applying the cutover checkpoint is idempotent: a second application (crash-recovery replay) produces byte-identical state and emits no duplicate receipts.
laws.crash_canaries: A crash (including kill-9) at any phase boundary must recover to a legal single-writer state derived from durable markers alone: a complete checkpoint implies embedded-canonical; anything less implies Convex-canonical. Acknowledged writes are never lost; zero-writer and two-writer recoveries are both violations.

checkpoint.note: The cutover checkpoint is ONE atomic artifact carrying every continuity anchor. A checkpoint missing any field group is incomplete and MUST refuse cutover.

field_semantics.signed_heads_high_water: The signedHeads high-water mark at cutover; the embedded store may never accept a head below it (rollback-attack fence).
field_semantics.attestation_nonces: The consumed attestation-nonce set digest; consumed nonces stay consumed across the cutover (no replay window).
field_semantics.tombstones_no_resurrection: The tombstone set digest; erased records stay erased in the embedded store (R44 no-resurrection carries over).
field_semantics.record_id_derivation: The content-addressed recordId derivation rule identity (R34), pinned so both stores derive identical IDs for identical content — the parity comparator's join key.
field_semantics.receipt_chain_anchors: The receipt-chain anchor digests at cutover, so the embedded receipt continuation chains from the exact Convex-era head.

transitions.CONVEX_CANONICAL->SHADOW_READS: Embedded follower attaches read-only. No write-path change.
transitions.SHADOW_READS->PARITY_PROVEN: Requires consecutive clean parity windows (full recordId-set equality AND per-record content-digest equality, both directions). Any mismatch resets to SHADOW_READS.
transitions.PARITY_PROVEN->CUTOVER_CHECKPOINT: Requires kill switch unset AND a complete checkpoint (all 5 field groups). Writes quiesce; checkpoint is written durably before the writer flips.
transitions.CUTOVER_CHECKPOINT->EMBEDDED_CANONICAL: Atomic writer flip. Convex demotes to read-only shadow (for the rollback window).
transitions.EMBEDDED_CANONICAL->CONVEX_CANONICAL: Rollback only, at the checkpoint boundary, per the rollback law.

out_of_scope_this_round: Any SQLite/libSQL dependency or embedded store implementation
Any provider call, Nebius arming, or Cell 0A interaction
DurableReceiptBridge wiring (its own future governed brick)
